author: hangleang
content: I'm sure Grandine emit `payload_attestation` SSE, but the spec expect `payload_attestation_message` as the topic. that could explain we are missing the event in the metric

author: lqzic
content:
Hello <@680234424037670912>, while inspecting Teku's `head_v2` emission path I noticed that `head` and `head_v2` events are logically bundled together - which results in a duplicate v1 `head` emission when the head's payload status changes.
https://github.com/Consensys/teku/blob/c905b16ae7f08634793f318442b5c0cbea2d41d3/data/beaconrestapi/src/main/java/tech/pegasys/teku/beaconrestapi/handlers/v1/events/EventSubscriptionManager.java#L142-L190
I'm working on the `head_v2` implementation in Lodestar, which is why I was taking a look at your implementation.

author: lqzic
content: putting here the `head_v2` implementation question for Teku & Prysm so it doesn't get lost

author: hangleang
content: we should only emit `head_v2` when head's payload status change from `empty` to `full`.  however, should we emit the event as well when payload status change from `full` to `empty`?

author: hangleang
content: e.g. when payload got reorg

author: lqzic
content: I think that Teku already emits a new event on every payload status change. I suppose that these events will be helpful to the builder entity more than the VCs. If that is the behavior we want then beacon-APIs description of head_v2 should get updated accordingly.

author: nflaig
content: the only way a payload can be reorged is by the next beacon block, meaning the head would change, imo it's questionable to me to even have the `payload_status` as part of the event at all, but I would like to hear more opinions why this is useful, and also how others have implemented this

author: nflaig
content: <@921369499733725205> is representing lodestar on this matter, he started working on an implementation

author: .paulharris
content: that'll be my bad, i probably wasn't fully across the subtlety there

author: .paulharris
content: i'll read up and see whats expected

author: .paulharris
content: iirc, if its firing twice then its changed its head - we've got two fork choice nodes so the head literally changes so maybe its just how it is... has anyone implemented that differently?

author: lqzic
content: Well, the only other available implementation atm is Prysm's. From what I've seen, they emit both events on head update, but only `head_v2` on payload import - which makes sense because head v1 can't reflect a payload status change anyways and that also prevents event duplication.

author: .paulharris
content: doesnt that mean head is incorrect any time the payload doesnt arrive? that doesnt sound correct either

author: nflaig
content: the prysm behavior is definitley wrong if that's how it works

author: .paulharris
content: starting to seem pointless having this v2 at all

author: .paulharris
content: so the main point was the dependent root meaning changes

author: .paulharris
content: https://github.com/ethereum/beacon-APIs/pull/590/changes so this whole discussion about payload available isn't really why it was even introduced

author: .paulharris
content: but also from description `The node may emit a second head event for the same beacon block and slot if there is an update in the `payload_status` from empty to full.`

author: .paulharris
content: so, it seems like its fine to emit both like teku does

author: nflaig
content: also to <@498331483732312075>'s point about the payload reorg, maybe he meant the following, you have `(B1, EMPTY)` and `(B1, FULL)`, then `get_head` for slot 1 would be `(B1, FULL)`, and for slot 2 `get_head` would be based on `should_extend_payload`, let's assume attsters vote for `(B1, EMPTY)` and slot 2 had no block, so in slot 3 `get_head` would be `(B1, EMPTY)` so the head flipped from `FULL` to `EMPTY` that is a reorg, but in regard to the `head` event, I don't think we would trigger that if we wanna match our current head (v1) emit pattern

author: .paulharris
content: so based on that pr, yes theres a fair point that the dependent root meaning is fundamentally different - so v2 is important.

author: nflaig
content: yes, that confuses me too, who and for what do we consume it, I doubt there is any usage from the vc side, at least right now I don't see it

author: .paulharris
content: to me teku complies with that description but i dont know who would be consuming the payload status

author: .paulharris
content: maybe a builder? dont know

author: nflaig
content: so for slot 1, before you receive the payload your head is `(B1, EMPTY)` then it changes to `(B1, FULL)` on payload import, but both nodes have 0 weight, so for current slot, what value does this bring that `execution_payload` not already solves?

author: .paulharris
content: maybe <@523051247884959744> or <@602753420033785856> have opinions, to me its inconsequential but i could definitely be missing something

author: nflaig
content:
there was an issue from potuz here https://github.com/ethereum/beacon-APIs/issues/609

the note worth pointing out is the following:
> we trigger a payload event but there is no signal as whether this payload was timely or not.

seems like potuz is saying this should consider timeliness, but just from the conversation I don't think that's what teku does, even if payload is untimely, you will emit the 2nd event since head still changes to `(B, FULL)` in current slot, is that right?

author: .paulharris
content: i wonder if ptc is not timely but we build or something weird i wonder if theres 3... not sure we have a test case like that

author: nflaig
content: for the `head_v2` you mean?

author: .paulharris
content: yeh if it re-emits if ptc is not timely and we saw a payload it'd have to emit again to say payload not present even though we might have seen it?

author: .paulharris
content: this seems all kinds of messy anyway if the field isnt even needed

author: nflaig
content: so for any use case the vc has, the `payload_status` is definitely not needed

author: nflaig
content: I am not sure how to interpret this https://github.com/ethereum/beacon-APIs/issues/609, but `whether this payload was timely or not` is fair point, we don't have such a event, unless I am missing something
